description_long: This object is a cylindrical metallic bomb with a weathered, industrial appearance, featuring a prominent radioactive warning symbol on its side. The body is primarily gray with yellow hazard markings and reinforced bands, suggesting it is designed for durability and visibility. There are two large, circular end caps, one of which has a raised hatch or access port, and the other is smooth and sealed. The bomb is supported by three rectangular feet, elevating it slightly above the ground, and has two sturdy lifting lugs on top for transport. A thin yellow cable or hose extends from one end, possibly for detonation or connection to another device. The surface is rough and worn, with visible grime and corrosion, indicating prolonged exposure to harsh environments.
description: A cylindrical, metallic bomb with yellow hazard markings, radioactive warning symbol, and a protruding cable. The surface is weathered and industrial, with reinforced bands and lifting lugs.
description_view_0: Side view showing the radioactive warning symbol, yellow hazard stripes, and a large circular hatch on the left end.
description_view_1: End view displaying the smooth, sealed metallic cap and the yellow cable extending outward.
description_view_2: Opposite side view with another radioactive warning symbol, yellow hazard stripes, and a smaller raised port on the right end.
description_view_3: End view showing the raised hatch or access port and two lifting lugs on top.
attribute_detonation_mechanism: A cable or hose extends from one end, possibly serving as a remote detonation trigger, and a hatch or port may provide access to internal mechanisms.
